text:
Admission Date:  1997-01-30              Discharge Date:   2014-10-05

Date of Birth:  1990-04-15             Sex:   M

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Jennifer
Chief Complaint:
1. DKA
2. Right foot ulcer

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
1. 1976-06-08: Incision and drainage of right foot ulcer

2. 2006-11-04: Debridement of right foot ulcer and closure of
operative wound


History of Present Illness:
CC:[**CC Contact Info 64235**]
HPI: Patient is a 23 year old male with diabetes who initially
presented to ER for evaluation of right foot pain that began on
1972-07-30. The patient was seen at Fairmont General Hospital several times for
this problem but was not satisfied with his care (pain control).
An ultrasound at Fairmont General Hospital was performed to rule out DVT. Patient does
not report any history of trauma or insect bite to the foot. In
the ED, evaluation of the patient revealed him to be in DKA.
Patient denied on admission any associated
fever,chills,nausea,vomiting.

The patient was admitted to the CCU with poorly controlled blood
sugar and foot infection. On admission the patient's blood sugar
was 449 with an anion gap of 30, Hgb A1C of 16.5. The patient
was hydrated with 4LNS,2L LR, on insulin gtt 6u decrased to 4U
once bs approximately 250, the patient was then started on
D51/2normal saline. Through the course of the day in the CCU the
patient's AG continued to close with hydration and insulin drip.
The patient was converted to regular 11/NPH 22. Patient now
tranferred to floor with most recent labs of blood sugar = 263,
anion gap 14.

Medications: None

Allergies: NKDA

A/P: Patient is a 23 year old male transferred from MICU care to
floor for poorly controlled diabetes, right foot infection
complicated by DKA.

1. DKA - Patient no longer on insulin drip, transferred to floor
with SQ Heparin.
- Hydration - continue to hydrate patient with normal saline
without D5, 125cc/hr
- continue patient with SQ NPH:30am, 14pm
- Continue to monitor anion gap with BMP tid
- Continue to check finger sticks qid
- continue Diabetic diet on floor
- continue wound care and diabetic education
- Atkinson eye consulted to see patient while in house
.
2. Right foot infection:
- patients right foot extremely tender to touch, erythematous,
worrisome in a diabetic for a fasciitis vs. simple cellulitis,
will additionally check CK
- patient started on Unasyn in MICU, will continue
- Flagyl added for additional anaerobe converage
- CT scan of foot pending to investigate for possible fasciitis
or infection requiring surgical intervention.
- patient given tetanus booster x1
.
3. Thrush - mycelex troches
4. FEN: Continue diabetic diet
5. CODE: Full
6. Ppx: None currently
6. Dispo: Pending

Past Medical History:
PMH: DM Type I vs. II?, poorly controlled
PSHx: None

Social History:
Patient denies any tobacco or ETOH use. Patient is a mental
health worker in 5102 Boyer Mill
South Brendanchester, VA 66588, he currently lives with his
father, sister, and grandmother.

Family History:
Family history positive for DM,CVA, cardiac disease
Anthony Jones mother died at age 45 from heart disease related to
diabetes
No family history of sickle cell disease


Physical Exam:
Vital signs: T= 98.5, BP-125/74, HR-86, RR=18, SpO2 98% on RA
Gen: Patient is a young black male lying in bed comfortably in
NAD
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. OP: MMM, + small white plaques under
tongue
Neck: Supple, no thyromegaly, no LAD, No JVD
Chest: Lungs CTA bilat ant and posterior
Abdomen: obese, soft, non-tender, non-distended. + Striae
Extrem: Patients right foot swollen and skin shiny to ankle.
Erythematous and tender to even gentle touch. DP pulses 2+
bilaterally. Patient with dry cracked skin and ulcerations
between toes on both feet.

Pertinent Results:
Radiology:
02-20: CT lower extremity: No focal abscess. Diffuse subcutaneous
edema most prominently involving the dorsal aspect of the foot
and extending along the medial aspect of the ankle. No evidence
of osteomyelitis.
.
10-30: 2.2 x 1.3 cm fluid collection at the distal right dorsal
foot concerning for abscess.
2. Edematous subcutaneous tissues overlying the right foot and
ankle.
3. Heterogenous likely inflammatory soft tissue mass overlying
dorsum of mid foot.
.
Labs:
On admission date:
1997-01-30 08:14AM   GLUCOSE-232* UREA N-4* CREAT-0.6 SODIUM-138
POTASSIUM-3.1* CHLORIDE-109* TOTAL CO2-14* ANION GAP-18
.
On discharge:
2015-11-14: WBC 6.4 HgB 11.5* Hct 34.2* Plt 376
Chem 7: Glu 186  BUN 13 Cr 0.6 Na 137 K 4.3 Cl 103 AG: 9


Brief Hospital Course:
1. DKA: Patient was transferred to our care from the MICU. On
admission to the MICU patient's BS = 449, with Anion gap - 16.5
(Hgb A1C = 16). The patient was found to have a right foot ulcer
and cellulitis, likely as the precipitating factor for his DKA.
Patient was hydrated with 4LNS,2L LR, on insulin gtt 6u
decreased to 4U once bs approximately 250,the patient was then
started on D51/2normal saline.  The patient's gap continued to
close and he was converted to regular 11/NPH 22. Patient was
then transferred to floor with most recent labs of blood sugar =
263, anion gap 14. The patient's insulin regimen was titrated up
with finger sticks qid, finally with a regimen of Insulin Lisp &
Lisp Prot (Hum) 75-25 unit/mL Suspension 44units in am, 22 units
in evening before dinner with NPH humalog sliding scale q 6hr.
.
2. Right foot ulcer: Patient admitted to our floor with right
foot pain. On admission the patient's foot ws significant for
edema, shiny turgid skin, and exquisite tenderness. Because of
concern for possible fasciitis or abscess, a CT and US were
performed. CT on 02-20 demontrated no evidence of abscess,
fasciitis, or osteo. However, over the course of admission the
patient's pain increased and an area of fluctuance could be
felt. On 10-30 a Right foot US was performed which demonstrated a
2.2 x 1.3 cm fluid collection at the distal right dorsal foot
concerning for abscess. The patient was taken to the OR on 10-30
under the care of Dr. David (podiatry) for I+D of the right foot
abscess. The patient was originally started on Unsayn on
admission. Cultures from the orignal unopened wound were
significant for pansensitive pseudomonas. The patient's abx
regimen was therefore changed from Unasyn to Levoquin and Zosyn.
The patient tolerated his surgery well and continued to be
afebrile with better pain control. On 05-27 the patient underwent
further planned debridement of the wound as well as surgical
closure. He again tolerated the procedure well. The patient was
seen by PT and instructed not to weight bear until his follow up
appointment with Dr. David in podiatry. The patient was
discharged with two additional weeks of abx therapy on
Clindamycin 300 mg PO qid and ciprofloxacin 500mg po bid.
.
3. Thrush: patient was noticed on admission to have small amount
of Thrush under his tongue, likely from his poor diabetic
control. The thrush resolved with Mycelex troches and the
patient was instructed on the importance of good glucose control
and follow up.
.
4. Follow up: The patient was given appointments to follow up
with Dr. David in podiatry, Dr. James at Atkinson Diabetes center,
and was additionally scheduled to see myself in [**Company 191**] Emergency Department
North Suite.


Medications on Admission:
None

Discharge Medications:
1. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: 1-2 Tablets PO every 04-17
hours as needed for pain.
2. Oxycodone 5 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO every 4-6 hours
as needed for pain: Hold for sedation or RR <12. Thanks.
Disp:*30 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
3. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO twice a
day for 28 doses.
Disp:*28 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
4. Clindamycin HCl 300 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO four
times a day for 56 doses.
Disp:*56 Capsule(s)* Refills:*0*
5. Insulin Lisp & Lisp Prot (Hum) 75-25 unit/mL Suspension Sig:
One (1) unit Subcutaneous see instructions: Please give 44units
in am before breakfast.
Please give 22 units in evening before dinner.  .
Disp:*3 bottles* Refills:*2*
6. Insulin Lispro (Human) 100 unit/mL Solution Sig: per sliding
scale units Subcutaneous four times a day: please see humalog
insulin sliding scale.
Disp:*2 bottles* Refills:*2*


Discharge Disposition:
Home With Service

Facility:
Saint Joseph Hospital Homecare

Discharge Diagnosis:
Primary:
1. Diabetic Ketoacidosis
2. Right Foot Ulcer

Secondary:
1. Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus


Discharge Condition:
Good. Patient is nonweight bearing on the right foot.

Discharge Instructions:
1. Please follow up all outpatient appointments

2. Patient should return to hospital for symptoms of
nausea/vomiting, dizziness, general malaise, loss of
consciousness or any other concerning symptoms.

3. Please take all medications as prescribed.

Followup Instructions:

COMPLEX) PODIATRY Phone:491-186-5292 Date/Time:1994-02-05 1:10.
Please call to confirm appointment date and time.

2. Provider Danielle Gomez, MD Where: Kaiser Permanente Los Angeles Medical Center
Brookhaven Hospital Phone:866-30-2893 Date/Time:2010-01-13 2:00

3. Atkinson Diabetes Center: You have an appointment with Dr. James
at Atkinson on 04-16 at 4:00 PM. However, you will need to be seen
before this in clinic next week. They are going to overbook you
an appointment and will call you with this time in the next
couple of days.
